title: Code Collective Knowledge Hub
text:
Hallo!
Code Collective (name TK) is a supportive community for critical–creative coding. It is organized as a collaboratory (colloquium-collaboration-laboratory) emphasizing co-learning and co-teaching, process, and experimentation. In this cooperative space you can dabble in code, troubleshoot a dream project, ask ‘silly’ questions, and explore new programming techniques suited to your artistic practice. The topics and tech will vary based on our shared interests, always with a focus on the sociotechnical impacts of our practice. All levels and languages are welcome!

title: Tech Resources
text:
Coding on the desktop/laptop
Here are some tools you might try out…
miniconda, Python environment and package manager
hyper, customizable terminal/console (there are many others)
VS Code, software for writing code (you can also use a text editor, but software like this means to help)
quarto, built this knowledge hub, a framework for presenting or publishing text and data in many formats, including interactively. For example, can run Python (Jupyter notebooks), R, Javascript (Observable), and Julia language cells embedded in web sites.
[git]

title: Tech Resources
text:
Running servers
Caddy, server, alt to nginx
Matrix, Conduit server self-host
Hetzner, Germany, cheap-ish, not entirely intuitive, some starters
Nearly Free Speech, cheapest, least intuitive
Scalingo, France, many starters
Vercel, US, serverless, many starters
Supabase, dev platform with database incl.

title: Tech Resources
text:
Working with ML models
Ollama, running AI models locally, no data sharing
Onyx or OpenWebUI
ComfyUI, visual interface for programming ML
Langchain, framework for quick AI agent coding
